label: Request Approval
description: Create an approval request and pause this scenario until a human approves or rejects. Use the callback URL from a Make webhook to resume your scenario after the decision.

expect.name: template_id
expect.label: Template
expect.help: Select a template to apply server-side defaults. Leave the title or callback URL blank if you want the template to supply them. Any field you fill in below will override the template.

expect.name: title
expect.label: What needs approval?
expect.help: A short description of what you want approved (e.g. 'Send invoice #1234 to client'). Leave blank to use the selected template title. If no template supplies one, OKRunit will use a generic Make title.

expect.name: description
expect.label: Details
expect.help: Additional context for the reviewer (optional). Use dynamic data from previous modules.

expect.name: callback_url
expect.label: Callback URL
expect.help: To receive the approval decision: create a second scenario with the OKRunit 'Approval Decision Received' trigger, copy the webhook URL, and paste it here.

expect.name: source_url
expect.label: Scenario URL
expect.help: Link back to this Make scenario. Use the built-in system variable {{scenarioUrl}} to auto-populate, or paste the URL manually. This enables an 'Open in Make' button in OKRunit.

expect.name: metadata
expect.label: Metadata (JSON)
expect.help: Optional JSON data to attach (e.g. {"order_id": "123"}). Routing rules, priority, expiration, and approvers are all configured in your OKRunit dashboard at okrunit.com.
